topic: WP07 — Envoy Gateway (gateway-helm) + standard Gateway API CRDs version pins

key_findings: Chart OCI ref is oci://docker.io/envoyproxy/gateway-helm (NOT oci://gateway-helm-charts/gateway-envoy).

Chart installs Envoy Gateway CRDs, Gateway API CRDs, and the safe-upgrade policy as a sub-chart by default (crds.enabled=true). To install Gateway API CRDs separately (operator decision: yes), pass --skip-crds and disable the safe upgrade policy: --skip-crds --set crds.gatewayAPI.safeUpgradePolicy.enabled=false.
Chart namespace is envoy-gateway-system; createNamespace=false by default so the bootstrap must pass --create-namespace.

Standard-channel CRDs are bundled at https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/gateway-api/releases/download/v1.6.0/standard-install.yaml (~20K lines, includes gatewayclasses, gateways, httproutes, referencegrants, grpcroutes GA, backendtlspolicies).
Apply with: kubectl apply --server-side -f <url> for idempotent re-apply.

Cilium 1.16.1 already runs with gatewayAPI.enabled=true and provides GAMMA L4 awareness; the upstream standard CRDs are still required because Cilium GAMMA does NOT install the standard CRD set, only listens for it.

registry_probes.decision: Align chart pin = app release version v1.8.2 (chart version == app release for this project; the chart's Chart.yaml appVersion mirrors the release tag).

registry_probes.result: 20,116 bytes, CustomResourceDefinition x8 (gatewayclasses, gateways, httproutes, referencegrants, grpcroutes, tlsroutes, backendtlspolicies + the v1alpha2 back-compat set).
registry_probes.decision: Pin CRDs URL = v1.6.0 standard channel. Apply via kubectl --server-side in a new bootstrap phase.

envoy_gateway_v1_8_2.rationale: From /envoyproxy/gateway install docs (context7 snippet): 'Ensure you have Helm v3+ installed' and 'Envoy Gateway requires Kubernetes 1.28 or later'. We run k3s v1.36.2+k3s1 which is well within range.

live_cluster.compatibility: PASS — k3s 1.36 > Envoy Gateway min 1.28; Cilium GAMMA + standard CRDs coexist because Cilium GAMMA only provides the validating webhook for the standard CRDs, not a competing GatewayClass implementation.

values_structure_v1_8_2.summary: Read from charts/gateway-helm/values.tmpl.yaml at tag v1.8.2 (180 lines).

key_paths.crds.enabled: true (chart installs Gateway API CRDs by default)
key_paths.crds.gatewayAPI.safeUpgradePolicy.enable: true (default; we DISABLE this when applying standard CRDs separately)
key_paths.service.type: ClusterIP (no LoadBalancer provisioner on Proxmox+k3s — same constraint as phase-2.md kind gotcha; not an issue because the data-plane Service is ClusterIP and the GitLab chart only needs an in-cluster Gateway address)

values_overrides_to_apply.deployment.replicas: 1 (already default; pin to make drift visible)
values_overrides_to_apply.config.envoyGateway.gateway.controllerNa: gateway.envoyproxy.io/gatewayclass-controller (chart default — keep explicit for the contract test)

decision_log.rationale: Canonical upstream OCI ref per context7 + chart annotations (org.opencontainers.image.source=https://github.com/envoyproxy/gateway). The alternative oci://gateway-helm-charts/gateway-envoy used by some blogs is NOT the upstream — would break pinning.
Latest stable on ghcr/docker.io as of 2026-07-08 (released 2026-07-01, two patch releases in 1.8.x line indicate active maintenance).

decision_log.decision: Standard CRDs pinned separately at v1.6.0 (operator decision: 'pin them')
decision_log.rationale: Operator explicitly chose to pin the upstream standard CRDs as a separate bootstrap step (plan §3.4 question 3 answered: 'pin them'). This means the chart's crds.enabled=false + crds.gatewayAPI.safeUpgradePolicy.enabled=false at install time. Bootstrap phase order: (1) kubectl apply standard CRDs, (2) helm install gateway-helm --skip-crds --set crds.gatewayAPI.safeUpgradePolicy.enabled=false.

decision_log.decision: Bootstrap phase order: standard CRDs → gateway-helm install → gateway_smoke
decision_log.rationale: CRDs must exist before any Gateway/HTTPRoute YAML is applied. The gateway-helm chart's own CRDs (EnvoyPatchPolicy, EnvoyProxy, BackendTrafficPolicy etc.) ship as a separate sub-chart `crds/` inside gateway-helm; those land on helm install. The bootstrap's gateway_smoke phase only needs the standard set to admit an HTTPRoute.

decision_log.decision: Both cicd + apps clusters install Envoy Gateway + CRDs (operator decision: 'yes both clusters are in scope')
decision_log.rationale: Both clusters need GatewayClass=envoy for the GitLab chart (cicd) and for any future apps-cluster ingress (apps). Single phase function, idempotent re-run.

decision_log.decision: GatewayClass name = envoy (default chart value, operator decision: 'use envoy')
decision_log.rationale: Matches GitLab chart's gatewayClassName=envoy expectation from blueprint phase-2.md §14 (NodePort data-plane exposure section). No override needed.

decision_log.decision: gateway_smoke uses ClusterIP data-plane Service (no LoadBalancer provisioner on Proxmox+k3s)
decision_log.rationale: Same constraint as blueprint phase-2.md §14 (NodePort gotcha). The cilium/chart default service.type=ClusterIP works. The smoke test curls the ClusterIP from inside the cluster, not the public hostname.

resources.name: proxmox-k8s-cicd-smoke
resources.rationale: All smoke-test resources live in this namespace so a re-run cleans them up with `kubectl delete ns proxmox-k8s-cicd-smoke`.

resources.name: echo
resources.rationale: Tiny echo server; returns the text as the body so curl can match exactly. hashicorp/http-echo is a 10MB image and is in the live cluster's containerd cache from prior runs.

resources.name: echo
resources.rationale: ClusterIP Service that selects the echo pods; Gateway backendRefs this.

resources.name: smoke-gw
resources.rationale: Probes whether GatewayClass=envoy exists and is admitted (the chart creates this GatewayClass on install).

resources.name: smoke
resources.rationale: Routes the listener to the echo Service.

assertions: kubectl get gatewayclass envoy returns controllerName=gateway.envoyproxy.io/gatewayclass-controller with status.condition[type=Accepted].status=True
kubectl get gateway smoke-gw -n proxmox-k8s-cicd-smoke returns status.conditions[type=Programmed].status=True within 60s
kubectl get httproute smoke -n proxmox-k8s-cicd-smoke returns status.parents[0].conditions[type=ResolvedRefs].status=True within 60s

resources.name: smoke-pvc
resources.rationale: Tiny PVC against the lvm-thin SC; 1Gi is the smallest sensible size for lvm-thin LV rounding.

resources.name: smoke-write
resources.rationale: First pass: write the marker file, then exit 0.

resources.name: smoke-read
resources.rationale: Second pass: re-mount the same PVC and assert the marker survives pod churn (proves NodePublishVolume unmount/remount worked).

assertions: kubectl get pvc smoke-pvc -n proxmox-k8s-cicd-smoke reaches Bound within 60s with volumeHandle starting with proxmox://bigbertha/
kubectl logs smoke-write -n proxmox-k8s-cicd-smoke contains the marker text
kubectl logs smoke-read -n proxmox-k8s-cicd-smoke returns the marker text and exit code 0

open_risks_for_live_apply: Cilium 1.16.1 GAMMA + Envoy Gateway: both register validating webhooks for the standard CRDs. If Cilium rejects an HTTPRoute that Envoy Gateway accepts, the gateway_smoke will fail. WP07 tests check for this in the live apply.
Standard CRDs v1.6.0 require Kubernetes 1.29+ for some fields. Cluster is k3s 1.36.2 → fine, but worth a quick kubectl version check at phase start.
proxmox-csi-plugin StorageClass may not be marked default on a fresh cluster; the csi_smoke phase asserts is-default-class=true before creating the PVC.
